Violin.text: I have studied to Grade 8 AMEB equivalent, sight-read easily in any key/rhythm, and can lead a complex string section (e.g. Ravel, Shostakovich).
I play confidently in ensembles, can lead or follow, read complex time signatures, but like to practice very complex fast runs.
I am comfortable with standard classical works, play regularly with a good tone, and can sight-read common keys (e.g. Early Mozart).
I have some experience with chamber playing, read simple meters safely, but sometimes lose my spot or dip out on very fast runs.
I am a beginner violinist, returning after a massive break, and prefer to prepare all notes thoroughly at a slow, supportive pace.

Cello.text: I read tenor/treble clef easily, can play thumb position, sight-read dynamically, and can lead a complex group.
I am confident across major keys, have good intonation, can follow a group leader safely, and sight-read easily.
I play regularly, have a solid basic sound, and can read simple keys on the fly. Good starting point for cellos.
I read bass clef fine but struggle with tenor clef. I sometimes dip out during very fast cello sections.
I am mostly a beginner, returning to the instrument, and require slow tempos and advance score preparation.

Piano.text: I have studied to Grade 8 or above, read complex modern rhythms, and can handle massive Romantic pieces (e.g. Brahms trios).
I can read complex multi-stave scores, have extensive ensemble experience, and can sight-read classical keys easily.
I can sight-read standard Baroque or early Classical pieces with reasonable accuracy but avoid dense Romantic pieces.
I struggle to sight-read and play at the same time. I need at least 1-3 weeks of advance practice to find notes.

Wind.text: Advanced player. Easily sight-read complex keys, transpositions, and keep a beautiful, stable, tuned tone.
Experienced wind player. Keep solid intonation, comfortable with complex modern signatures.
Intermediate. Can play standard classical works but prefer to practice challenging passages in advance.
Beginner/Returning player. Struggle to sight-read and keep tempo on the fly.
